title: Museum of Light & Strategy: Interactive Exhibit Builder
description: The user curates a virtual museum exhibit featuring three artifacts: a lantern, a chess set, and an antique camera. They must position each artifact on designated pedestals, adjust spotlight intensity and color for each display, rotate artifacts to their best viewing angle, and finally capture a virtual photograph using the antique camera to complete the exhibit. A validation panel tracks all setup steps.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 that implements an interactive museum exhibit builder. Load three GLB models: Lantern.glb, ABeautifulGame.glb (chess set), and AntiqueCamera.glb. Scene setup: a dark museum room made from a large box geometry with dark gray interior walls and a reflective floor plane. Place three circular pedestals (cylinder geometry, marble-textured) spaced evenly in a row. Add soft ambient light at 0.15 intensity and one SpotLight per pedestal initially turned off. Camera: perspective camera with OrbitControls, initial position viewing all three pedestals. Interaction steps requiring validation: (1) Drag-and-drop the Lantern model onto the left pedestal using raycasting — validate it snaps within 0.5 units of pedestal center. (2) Drag the chess set onto the center pedestal with same snap validation. (3) Drag the antique camera onto the right pedestal. (4) Click each pedestal spotlight toggle button in an HTML overlay panel to activate its SpotLight — validate all three are on. (5) Use a color picker input for each spotlight to set unique colors — validate that no two spotlights share the same color. (6) Use a slider (range 0.5 to 3.0) to adjust each spotlight intensity — validate all three have been adjusted from default. (7) Click each artifact to rotate it 45 degrees on Y-axis to its display angle — validate all three have been rotated. (8) Press a Capture Exhibit button that switches the view to the antique camera model's forward-facing direction, renders a snapshot effect with a brief white flash overlay, and displays a thumbnail of the captured view. Display a progress tracker panel showing steps 1-8 with checkmarks. Each step must be completed in order. Use dat.gui or simple HTML controls. Add subtle particle dust in the air using Points geometry for atmosphere.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  lanternPedestalDistance: number,         // distance in units between lantern model and left pedestal center
  chessPedestalDistance: number,         // distance in units between chess set model and center pedestal center
  cameraPedestalDistance: number,         // distance in units between camera model and right pedestal center
  spotlightsActiveCount: number,         // count of spotlights currently turned on (0-3)
  uniqueSpotlightColors: number,         // count of unique spotlight colors assigned (0-3)
  lanternSpotlightIntensity: number,         // intensity value of lantern spotlight (0.5-3.0)
  chessSpotlightIntensity: number,         // intensity value of chess spotlight (0.5-3.0)
  cameraSpotlightIntensity: number,         // intensity value of camera spotlight (0.5-3.0)
  lanternRotationY: number,         // Y-axis rotation of lantern model in radians
  allArtifactsPlaced: boolean,         // true when all three artifacts are on their pedestals
  exhibitComplete: boolean,         // true when all setup steps are validated and complete
}
